description:
You are working as a performance engineer who has just been asked for a quick, written snapshot of how much disk space each first-level project directory is consuming.  
The directories of interest are located in:

/home/user/projects

Within that parent directory, every immediate sub-directory represents a separate project (e.g., “app_alpha”, “app_beta”, etc.).  
Produce a **plain-text** log file named:

/home/user/disk_usage_report.log

containing an easily-parsable summary that meets **all** of the requirements below:

1.  Header line exactly (case-sensitive):
        Summary Report - Disk Usage by Project

2.  Second line must be exactly 60 hyphens ( “-” characters ).

3.  A table with one row per first-level sub-directory of /home/user/projects.  
    • Each row must have exactly two columns separated by a single tab character.  
    • Column 1 = directory name (not the full path).  
    • Column 2 = the directory’s size in whole megabytes, **rounded down** (e.g., “6”, not “6.2”).  
    • Rows must be sorted in descending order by size (largest project first).

4.  After the table, insert another identical 60-hyphen separator line.

5.  Final line with this precise label text followed by a space, a colon, another space, and the total size of /home/user/projects in whole MB:
        Total size of /home/user/projects : <NUMBER>

6.  Every line must end with UNIX line endings (LF), and the log must not contain trailing blank lines.

The tasks you must perform (exact commands are up to you, but expect to need only 3-4):

• Determine the disk usage of every first-level sub-directory under /home/user/projects in MB.  
• Sort the list as required.  
• Calculate the cumulative size of /home/user/projects in MB.  
• Write the information to /home/user/disk_usage_report.log using the exact format above.

Once finished, you can cat the file to the console so the supervising script sees the output, but the definitive grading will read /home/user/disk_usage_report.log directly.
truth:
Initial filesystem state (already present before the agent begins):

/home/user/projects/app_alpha/
    ├── bin/                    (1 MiB)
    ├── log/                    (3 MiB)
    └── README.md               (4 KiB, negligible)

Total for app_alpha  = 4 MiB (rounded down: 4)

/home/user/projects/app_beta/
    ├── data/                   (1 MiB)
    └── docs/                   (1 MiB)
Total for app_beta   = 2 MiB (rounded down: 2)

/home/user/projects/app_gamma/
    ├── src/                    (4 MiB)
    ├── tests/                  (1 MiB)
    └── report.pdf              (1 MiB)
Total for app_gamma  = 6 MiB (rounded down: 6)

Grand total of /home/user/projects = 12 MiB (rounded down: 12)

Expected contents of /home/user/disk_usage_report.log after successful completion (with “\n” indicating a LF newline character):

Summary Report - Disk Usage by Project\n
------------------------------------------------------------\n
app_gamma\t6\n
app_alpha\t4\n
app_beta\t2\n
------------------------------------------------------------\n
Total size of /home/user/projects : 12\n

There is no final trailing LF after the last “12\n”.

The automated test will:

1. Verify that /home/user/disk_usage_report.log exists.
2. Confirm every byte matches the expected content above.
3. Ensure file permissions are 644 (rw-r--r--) or more permissive.
name: task_000000_6e9092c2
